title: Pronunciation Practice 2: Ma, Ya, Ra, Wa Rows
description: Complete your core Hiragana pronunciation practice with the final consonant rows.

blocks.title: The Final Rows of Hiragana
points: Ma-row: ま(ma), み(mi), む(mu), め(me), も(mo) - Like 'm' in English.
Ya-row: や(ya), ゆ(yu), よ(yo) - Note there is no 'yi' or 'ye'.

Ra-row: ら(ra), り(ri), る(ru), れ(re), ろ(ro) - CRITICAL: The Japanese 'R' is not English 'R' or 'L'. It's a light tap behind the top teeth, kind of like an English 'D' or a Spanish rolled 'r' (but just one tap).
Wa-row: わ(wa), を(wo), ん(n) - を(wo) is usually pronounced just 'o', and ん is the only consonant that can end a syllable alone.

blocks.title: The Japanese 'R' Sound
blocks.instruction: The 'R' row is the hardest for English speakers. Pay close attention.

pairs.diff: Don't say 'ree', say a very light 'dee' or 'lee'.

words.note: Book. ho-n. Hold the 'n' for a full beat.

items.statement: The Japanese 'R' is exactly the same as the English 'R'.
items.explanation: It's totally different! It's closer to an 'L' or 'D' in English.

items.statement: The character 'ん' (n) takes up a whole beat of sound.
items.explanation: Yes. 'HON' is two beats: HO + N.

items.statement: There is a character for 'yi' in modern Japanese.
items.explanation: No, the Ya-row only has や(ya), ゆ(yu), and よ(yo).
